title: Reframe as discrete-time hazard plus optional time-to-pit auxiliary target
summary: Next-lap pit probability is a discrete hazard: probability of stopping in interval t+1 given survival until t.
rationale: Next-lap pit probability is a discrete hazard: probability of stopping in interval t+1 given survival until t. Discrete-time survival literature explicitly supports using standard classifiers on person-period data, and recent F1 pit-stop work reports benefit from coupling next-lap classification with a horizon-to-stop regression head.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary target like `laps_until_next_pit` within each driver-race sequence, train either a joint two-head model or two separate models, then feed the auxiliary prediction back into the final binary model. Keep labels lag-safe by computing future stop distance only from the target sequence, not test-time future rows.
expected_effect: Should sharpen pit-window discrimination, especially for medium-risk rows where current features know a stop is coming soon but not whether it is exactly next lap.
risk: More label engineering complexity; if not done strictly within train folds it can leak future information and inflate CV.
